emergent_themes.description: Kitchen sharing logistics and space constraints as primary delivery triggers, distinct from general convenience
Willingness to switch platforms for minimal price differences (£1), indicating extreme price sensitivity
Resistance to grocery delivery based on lack of social proof rather than rational evaluation
Willingness to pay delivery premiums for cuisines unavailable at home location
Systematic decision-making approach with surprising awareness gaps and irrational biases

coding_summary: Sean demonstrates extreme price sensitivity and systematic comparison shopping behavior, switching platforms for £1 differences while showing no brand loyalty. Kitchen logistics stress from shared living is the primary delivery trigger, not general convenience. Strong social proof bias prevents grocery delivery adoption despite rational benefits. Greek food obsession represents emotional attachment to unavailable cuisines. Post-graduation expectations show reduced usage due to limited options at home location.
